columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Darolutamide (n = 652)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 248 (38.0%), Grade 4: 183 (28.1%), Grade 5 (death): 27 (4.1%). Total: 248 + 183 + 27 = 458 patients. Total percentage: 38.0% + 28.1% + 4.1% = 70.2%. Value: 458 (70.2%)
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Placebo (n = 650)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 232 (35.7%), Grade 4: 181 (27.8%), Grade 5 (death): 26 (4.0%). Total: 232 + 181 + 26 = 439 patients. Total percentage: 35.7% + 27.8% + 4.0% = 67.5%. Value: 439 (67.5%)
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control

columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 reports "Grade 5 (death)" under "Worst grade" for "Any AE", which is generally considered all-cause treatment-emergent. It does not specify if these Grade 5 events are "treatment-related". Footnote 'd' for Table 3 states "Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.", implying these are all-cause.
